questions.question: What can you earn by completing learning paths in Notion Academy?

options.explanation: Certificates are only awarded for the Notion Certified Admin program, not for completing general learning paths.
Completing paths awards badges, as stated in the "Earn badges with Notion" section.
Notion Academy does not use a credit system for its courses.
Discounts are not mentioned as a reward for finishing learning paths.

questions.question: Which certification does Notion Academy offer for administrators?

options.explanation: Developer certification is not listed in this article.
The article explicitly mentions becoming a "Notion Certified Admin".
Design certification is not part of the described offerings.
Analyst certification is not mentioned.

questions.question: Which of the following is a beginner‑level featured lesson?

options.explanation: Workspace design is listed as an Advanced lesson.
Custom Agents is labeled as Beginner in the lesson list.
This lesson is Intermediate, not Beginner.
Templates & marketplace is an Intermediate lesson.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the Essentials Path?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path contains more than two lessons.
The article states the Essentials Path has 3 lessons.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Essentials Path.
Five lessons are part of the Advanced Path.

questions.question: Which featured lesson is described as helping automate repeat work and can run on a schedule or respond to triggers?

options.explanation: The description of Custom Agents mentions automation, schedules, and triggers.
Project management focuses on setting up projects and tasks, not automation.
That lesson is about teamwork, not automation.
Workspace design covers best‑practice layout, not automated agents.

questions.question: Which intermediate‑level lesson focuses on building an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The lesson description mentions creating an intake system with forms, charts, and automations.
That lesson is about scaling knowledge, not specifically an intake system.
Templates & marketplace deals with creating and selling templates, not intake systems.
Workspace design is an Advanced lesson about layout best practices.

questions.question: Which learning path is designed for users who want to create connected systems that unite teams and projects with the help of AI?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is aimed at beginners and does not focus on AI‑driven workflows.
The Workflows Path description mentions creating connected systems with AI.
Advanced Path covers automation and architecture but the AI‑focused description belongs to Workflows Path.
No such path is listed in the article.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn best practices for designing a workspace, including block selection and database organization, which featured lesson should they take?

options.explanation: Workspace design is explicitly described as teaching best practices for choosing blocks and organizing databases.
Building basics covers navigation and creating blocks, but not the deeper design best practices.
That lesson focuses on creating and selling templates, not workspace design.
Custom Agents is about automation, not workspace layout.

questions.question: Which featured lesson does NOT list a specific subject category (e.g., Essentials, Workflows) in its description?

options.explanation: Unlike other lessons, Templates & marketplace shows only level and type, without a category tag.
This lesson is tagged under Essentials.
Project management includes the Workflows category.
That lesson also carries the Workflows tag.

questions.question: Which path is labeled as "Advanced" and contains the most lessons among the three Badged Paths?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is Beginner and has only 3 lessons.
Workflows Path is Intermediate and includes 4 lessons.
Advanced Path is the only Advanced‑labeled path and has 5 lessons, the highest count.
No such path is listed in the article.
